revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大東神社 (南大東村)]]
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Daitō Shrine |image = File:南大東島の大東神社 その２.jpg |location = {{ill|Okinawa Prefecture|en|Okinawa Prefecture|qid=Q766445|lt=Okinawa Prefecture|1=Okinawa Prefecture}}, {{ill|Shimajiri District, Okinawa|en|Shimajiri District, Okinawa|qid=Q1206950|lt=Shimajiri District, Okinawa|1=Shimajiri District, Okinawa}}, {{ill|Minamidaitō, Okinawa|en|Minamidaitō, Okinawa|qid=Q1351200|lt=Minamidaitō|1=Minamidaitō, Okinawa}}, Aza Ikenozawa |coordinates ={{Coord|25.8425|N|131.226556|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity = {{ill|Amaterasu|qid=Q455602|1=Amaterasu}} |shrine_Rank = |founded = Meiji 33 (1900) |other_names = |annual_festival = September 23 (Hōnen Festival) |map = Japan Okinawa |location = {{ill|Minamidaito|qid=Q1351200|1=Minamidaitō, Okinawa}} }} [[File:南大東島 (139).JPG|thumb|200px|A monument within Daitō Shrine grounds indicating the habitat of the {{ill|Daitō Megabat|ja|ダイトウオオコウモリ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daitō Megabat|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to クビワオオコウモリ|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to クビワオオコウモリ|1=Ryukyu flying fox|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q575969}}]] '''Daitō Shrine''' (大東神社, ''Daitō Jinja'') is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Minamidaitō, Okinawa|en|Minamidaitō, Okinawa|qid=Q1351200|lt=Minamidaitō|1=Minamidaitō, Okinawa}}, {{ill|Shimajiri District, Okinawa|en|Shimajiri District, Okinawa|qid=Q1206950|lt=Shimajiri District, Okinawa|1=Shimajiri District, Okinawa}}, {{ill|Okinawa Prefecture|en|Okinawa Prefecture|qid=Q766445|lt=Okinawa Prefecture|1=Okinawa Prefecture}}, Japan. == deity == The shrine enshrines {{ill|Amaterasu|en|Amaterasu|qid=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|1=Amaterasu}}, the same as {{ill|Daitō-gū|ja|大東宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daitō-gū|qq=}} in {{ill|Kitadaitō, Okinawa|en|Kitadaitō, Okinawa|qid=Q1207554|lt=Kitadaitō|1=Kitadaitō, Okinawa}}. == History == In Meiji 33 (1900), when the {{ill|Daitō Islands|en|Daitō Islands|qid=Q697792|lt=Daitō Islands|1=Daitō Islands}} development group, led by {{ill|Hanzaemon Tamaki|ja|玉置半右衛門|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hanzaemon Tamaki|1=Tamaoki Han'emon|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11572749}}, began settlement operations on {{ill|Minamidaitōjima|en|Minamidaitōjima|qid=Q1204188|lt=Minamidaitō|1=Minamidaitōjima}}, the shrine was established by Tamaki Shōkai, which was involved in sugar production. Near the shrine is {{ill|Gonzō Pond|ja|権蔵池|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gonzō Pond|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the first freshwater pond discovered on the island by Okinoyama Gonzō, a member of the settlement group. == Festivals == * '''{{ill|Reisai|ja|例祭|fr|lt＝Annual Festival|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reisai|qq=}}''' – Held from '''September 22 to September 23''', known as the '''Minamidaitō Hōnen Festival''' (南大東村豊年祭). The main festival takes place on the 23rd. Unlike most of {{ill|Okinawa Prefecture|en|Okinawa Prefecture|qid=Q766445|lt=Okinawa Prefecture|1=Okinawa Prefecture}}, this festival features a parade of {{ill|Mikoshi|ja|御輿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikoshi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神輿|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神輿}} and {{ill|Matsuri float|en|Matsuri float|qid=Q11470974|lt=Dashi (float)|1=Matsuri float}}, with participation from various island communities and businesses. It is a unique blend of traditions from immigrants from {{ill|Hachijō-jima|en|Hachijō-jima|qid=Q1191120|lt=Hachijō Island|1=Hachijō-jima}} and other regions of Okinawa. The main festival includes a ritual sumo event, featuring both {{ill|Okinawan sumo|ja|沖縄角力|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okinawan sumo|1=Okinawa Sumo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q97167457}} and {{ill|Edo-period sumo|ja|江戸相撲|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Edo-period sumo|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, with matches for children, middle school students, and local young men, known for their intensity. The evening features performances such as {{ill|Daitō taiko|ja|大東大鼓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daitō taiko|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and Okinawan folk performances. == Natural Environment == The shrine's forest is home to nocturnal species such as the {{ill|Ryūkyū scops owl|en|Ryūkyū scops owl|qid=Q716317|lt=Ryukyu Scops Owl|1=Ryūkyū scops owl}} and the {{ill|Daitō Megabat|ja|ダイトウオオコウモリ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daitō Megabat|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to クビワオオコウモリ|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to クビワオオコウモリ|1=Ryukyu flying fox|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q575969}}, a species designated as a '''National Natural Monument'''. The area also contains a population of {{ill|Daitō Shirodamo|ja|ダイトウシロダモ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daitō Shirodamo|1=Neolitsea sericea var. argentea|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11316810}}, listed as '''Critically Endangered (CR)''' on Japan’s {{ill|Ministry of the Environment Red List|ja|環境省レッドリスト|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ministry of the Environment Red List|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to レッドリスト|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to レッドリスト|1=IUCN Red List|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q32059}}. == Related Shrines == * {{ill|Shinmei shrines|en|Shinmei shrines|qid=Q11590310|lt=Shimmei Shrine|1=Shinmei shrines}} * In Minamidaitō, besides Daitō Shrine, the following shrines exist: ** {{ill|Kotohira shrines|lt=Kotohira shrines|lt=Kotohira Shrine|ja|金比羅宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotohira Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 金刀比羅神社|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 金刀比羅神社}} (Annual Festival: October 10) ** {{ill|Akiba Shrines|ja|秋葉神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akiha Shrine|qq=}} (Annual Festival: November 16) {{Shinto-stub|qq=}} {{Okinawan Shrines|qq=}}
{{translated page|ja|大東神社 (南大東村)|version=98046034|comment=Imported full ja history}}
[[ja:大東神社 (南大東村)]]
